parts: Tragacantha bracteosa, also known as the common horsebean or Tragacanth, is a plant native to certain parts of the world.  The medicinal parts of Tragacantha bracteosa are primarily its roots and seeds, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various purposes. The roots of the plant are a rich source of tragacanth, a gum-like substance used as a natural adhesive and in some pharmaceutical applications. Tragacanth has also been used to soothe digestive issues and as a thickening agent in various medicines. The seeds of the plant, on the other hand, are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and have been used to treat respiratory issues and other health conditions.

intro:
There is limited information regarding the plant Tragacantha bracteosa. However, I can provide general information about the Tragacantha genus. The genus Tragacantha, also known as Astragalus, includes a large group of plants commonly known as milkvetches, which are a type of perennial or annual legume. 

Astragalus species, such as Tragacantha bracteosa, have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, helping to boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.

Bioactive constituents of Astragalus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which contribute to their medicinal properties. 

Astragalus is typically prepared as a tea, capsules, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
